Description: Verify login creates session and allows access to protected endpoints
Test Procedure: 1. POST /api/login with valid credentials 2. Extract JSESSIONID cookie 3. GET /api/me with cookie
Expected Result: Login returns 200 OK with JSESSIONID cookie and /api/me returns user details with role

Description: Verify protected endpoints reject unauthenticated requests
Test Procedure: 1. GET /api/me without session cookie 2. GET /api/customers without cookie
Expected Result: All requests return 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden

Test Case: Role-based access control - Staff blocked from Admin endpoints
Description: Verify Staff cannot access Admin-only endpoints
Test Procedure: 1. Login as Staff 2. Attempt GET /api/users 3. Attempt POST /api/users
Expected Result: 403 Forbidden for all Admin endpoints

Test Case: Role-based access control - Admin blocked from operational modules
Description: Verify Admin cannot access customer account and card modules
Test Procedure: 1. Login as Admin 2. Attempt GET /api/customers 3. Attempt GET /api/accounts 4. Attempt GET /api/cards
Expected Result: 403 Forbidden for all operational endpoints

Description: Verify full maker-checker workflow for customer creation
Test Procedure: 1. Staff login and create customer creating PENDING request 2. Manager login and approve the request 3. Verify customer exists in database
Expected Result: Customer record created in database with status ACTIVE after approval

Description: Verify a user cannot approve their own request
Test Procedure: 1. Manager login and create a customer 2. Same Manager tries to approve the request
Test Data: Same Manager user for both actions
Expected Result: Error: Maker cannot approve own request and request remains PENDING

Description: Verify rejection requires a reason and stores it
Test Procedure: 1. Staff creates a pending request 2. Manager rejects with reason 3. Staff views rejected request
Expected Result: Request status changes to REJECTED with rejection reason stored and visible to maker

Description: Verify rejected request can be edited and resubmitted with old request superseded
Test Procedure: 1. Staff views rejected request 2. Staff edits and resubmits 3. Check old request status
Expected Result: New PENDING request created and old request automatically marked as SUPERSEDED

Description: Verify email is sent to customer when account is activated
Test Procedure: 1. Staff creates account activation request 2. Manager approves 3. Check Brevo email delivery
Expected Result: Account activated in database and email sent to customer via Brevo API with activation details

Description: Verify email is sent with masked card number when card is issued
Test Procedure: 1. Staff creates card issuance request 2. Manager approves 3. Check email delivery
Expected Result: Card status changes to ISSUED and email sent with masked card number e.g. 1234 **** **** 5678

Test Case: Email notification on password reset approval
Description: Verify temporary password is generated and emailed on reset
Test Procedure: 1. Admin submits password reset for user 2. Admin approves the request 3. User receives email with temp password
Expected Result: Temporary password generated and emailed and user forced to change password on next login

Description: Verify existing cleartext data is auto-encrypted when application starts
Test Procedure: 1. Insert cleartext sensitive data directly in database 2. Start Spring Boot application 3. Query database after startup
Test Data: Cleartext ID number: 123456789012 in customers table
Expected Result: All cleartext fields encrypted to Base64 format and application logs show migration completed

Description: Verify complete two-factor authentication login process
Test Procedure: 1. Enable 2FA for user from Profile 2. Logout 3. Login with password receiving temp token 4. Enter TOTP code
Test Data: User with 2FA enabled and valid authenticator app
Expected Result: Password accepted with temporary token returned then TOTP verified and full session created

Description: Verify chatbot receives real-time system data and answers accurately
Test Procedure: 1. Create customers and accounts in system 2. Open chat widget 3. Ask How many customers do we have
Expected Result: Chatbot responds with accurate count matching actual database records

Test Case: Admin approve user request but blocked from operational requests
Description: Verify Admin can only approve USER-type pending requests
Test Procedure: 1. Login as Admin 2. Approve a USER-type pending request 3. Attempt to approve a CUSTOMER-type request
Test Data: USER pending request, CUSTOMER pending request
Expected Result: USER request approved successfully and CUSTOMER request returns 403: Admin can only approve user-related requests

Description: Verify complete card lifecycle from creation through all status transitions
Test Procedure: 1. Create card approved to INACTIVE 2. Issue card INACTIVE to ISSUED 3. Activate card ISSUED to ACTIVE 4. Block card ACTIVE to BLOCKED 5. Deactivate card
Expected Result: Each status transition creates pending request and all approved transitions update card status correctly with emails sent at issue and activation

Test Case: Frontend route guard and backend RBAC alignment
Description: Verify Angular guards and Spring Security rules enforce same access control
Test Procedure: 1. Login as Staff and manually navigate to /roles in browser 2. Login as Admin and navigate to /customers
Expected Result: Staff redirected from /roles with Access Denied and Admin redirected from /customers

Description: Verify passwords are hashed with BCrypt and never stored as plaintext
Test Procedure: 1. Create new user via Admin 2. Query database for user record 3. Compare stored password
Expected Result: Database stores BCrypt hash starting with $2a$ and raw password not found in database

Description: Verify system handles multiple pending requests without conflicts
Test Procedure: 1. Staff creates 3 different pending requests 2. Manager approves request 1 3. Manager rejects request 2 4. Request 3 remains pending
Test Data: 3 separate pending requests for different entities
Expected Result: Each request processed independently with correct statuses and no data corruption

Test Case: Data masking for encrypted fields in API response
Description: Verify sensitive fields are encrypted in database but masked in API responses
Test Procedure: 1. Create customer with ID number and phone 2. Query database directly to verify encryption 3. Call GET /api/customers to check API response
Expected Result: Database stores Base64-encoded AES-256-GCM encrypted values and API returns masked values e.g. ****9012 and ****6789
